If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→use std::cell::RefCell;\n     6→\n     7→use cssparser::SourceLocation;\n     8→use servo_arc::Arc;\n     9→use style::context::QuirksMode;\n    10→use style::error_reporting::{ContextualParseError, ParseErrorReporter};\n    11→use style::media_queries::MediaList;\n    12→use style::shared_lock::SharedRwLock;\n    13→use style::stylesheets::{\n    14→    AllowImportRules, Origin, Stylesheet, StylesheetInDocument, UrlExtraData,\n    15→};\n    16→use url::Url;\n    17→\n    18→#[derive(Debug)]\n    19→struct CSSError {\n    20→    pub url: Arc<Url>,\n    21→    pub line: u32,\n    22→    pub column: u32,\n    23→    pub message: String,\n    24→}\n    25→\n    26→struct TestingErrorReporter {\n    27→    errors: RefCell<Vec<CSSError>>,\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→impl TestingErrorReporter {\n    31→    pub fn new() -> Self {\n    32→        TestingErrorReporter {\n    33→            errors: RefCell::new(Vec::new()),\n    34→        }\n    35→    }\n    36→\n    37→    fn assert_messages_contain(&self, expected_errors: &[(u32, u32, &str)]) {\n    38→        let errors = self.errors.borrow();\n    39→        for (i, (error, &(line, column, message))) in errors.iter().zip(expected_errors).enumerate()\n    40→        {\n    41→            assert_eq!(\n    42→                (error.line, error.column),\n    43→                (line, column),\n    44→                \"line/column numbers of the {}th error: {:?}\",\n    45→                i + 1,\n    46→                error.message\n    47→            );\n    48→            assert!(\n    49→                error.message.contains(message),\n    50→                \"{:?} does not contain {:?}\",\n    51→                error.message,\n    52→                message\n    53→            );\n    54→        }\n    55→        if errors.len() < expected_errors.len() {\n    56→            panic!(\"Missing errors: {:#?}\", &expected_errors[errors.len()..]);\n    57→        }\n    58→        if errors.len() > expected_errors.len() {\n    59→            panic!(\"Extra errors: {:#?}\", &errors[expected_errors.len()..]);\n    60→        }\n    61→    }\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→impl ParseErrorReporter for TestingErrorReporter {\n    65→    fn report_error(\n    66→        &self,\n    67→        url: &UrlExtraData,\n    68→        location: SourceLocation,\n    69→        error: ContextualParseError,\n    70→    ) {\n    71→        self.errors.borrow_mut().push(CSSError {\n    72→            url: url.0.clone(),\n    73→            line: location.line,\n    74→            column: location.column,\n    75→            message: error.to_string(),\n    76→        })\n    77→    }\n    78→}\n    79→\n    80→#[test]\n    81→fn test_report_error_stylesheet() {\n    82→    let css = r\"\n    83→    div {\n    84→        background-color: red;\n    85→        display: invalid;\n    86→        background-image: linear-gradient(0deg, black, invalid, transparent);\n    87→        invalid: true;\n    88→    }\n    89→    @media (min-width: 10px invalid 1000px) {}\n    90→    @font-face { src: url(), invalid, url(); }\n    91→    @counter-style foo { symbols: a 0invalid b }\n    92→    @font-feature-values Sans Sans { @foo {} @swash { foo: 1 invalid 2 } }\n    93→    @invalid;\n    94→    @media screen { @invalid; }\n    95→    @supports (color: green) and invalid and (margin: 0) {}\n    96→    @keyframes foo { from invalid {} to { margin: 0 invalid 0; } }\n    97→    \";\n    98→    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    99→    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n   100→\n   101→    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n   102→    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n   103→    Stylesheet::from_str(\n   104→        css,\n   105→        url.clone().into(),\n   106→        Origin::UserAgent,\n   107→        media,\n   108→        lock,\n   109→        None,\n   110→        Some(&error_reporter),\n   111→        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n   112→        AllowImportRules::Yes,\n   113→    );\n   114→\n   115→    error_reporter.assert_messages_contain(&[\n   116→        (\n   117→            3,\n   118→            18,\n   119→            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'display: invalid;'\",\n   120→        ),\n   121→        (\n   122→            4,\n   123→            43,\n   124→            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'background-image:\",\n   125→        ), // FIXME: column should be around 56\n   126→        (5, 17, \"Unsupported property declaration: 'invalid: true;'\"),\n   127→        (7, 28, \"Invalid media rule\"),\n   128→        // When @counter-style is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n   129→        (9, 19, \"Invalid rule: '@counter-style \"),\n   130→        // When @font-feature-values is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n   131→        (10, 25, \"Invalid rule: '@font-feature-values \"),\n   132→        (11, 13, \"Invalid rule: '@invalid'\"),\n   133→        (12, 29, \"Invalid rule: '@invalid'\"),\n   134→        (13, 34, \"Invalid rule: '@supports \"),\n   135→        (14, 26, \"Invalid keyframe rule: 'from invalid '\"),\n   136→        (\n   137→            14,\n   138→            52,\n   139→            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'margin: 0 invalid 0;'\",\n   140→        ),\n   141→    ]);\n   142→\n   143→    assert_eq!(*error_reporter.errors.borrow()[0].url, url);\n   144→}\n   145→\n   146→#[test]\n   147→fn test_no_report_unrecognized_vendor_properties() {\n   148→    let css = r\"\n   149→    div {\n   150→        -o-background-color: red;\n   151→        _background-color: red;\n   152→        -moz-background-color: red;\n   153→    }\n   154→    \";\n   155→    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n   156→    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n   157→\n   158→    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n   159→    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n   160→    Stylesheet::from_str(\n   161→        css,\n   162→        url.into(),\n   163→        Origin::UserAgent,\n   164→        media,\n   165→        lock,\n   166→        None,\n   167→        Some(&error_reporter),\n   168→        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n   169→        AllowImportRules::Yes,\n   170→    );\n   171→\n   172→    error_reporter.assert_messages_contain(&[(\n   173→        4,\n   174→        31,\n   175→        \"Unsupported property declaration: '-moz-background-color: red;'\",\n   176→    )]);\n   177→}\n   178→\n   179→#[test]\n   180→fn test_source_map_url() {\n   181→    let tests = vec![\n   182→        (\"\", None),\n   183→        (\n   184→            \"/*# sourceMappingURL=something */\",\n   185→            Some(\"something\".to_string()),\n   186→        ),\n   187→    ];\n   188→\n   189→    for test in tests {\n   190→        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n   191→        let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n   192→        let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n   193→        let stylesheet = Stylesheet::from_str(\n   194→            test.0,\n   195→            url.into(),\n   196→            Origin::UserAgent,\n   197→            media,\n   198→            lock.clone(),\n   199→            None,\n   200→            None,\n   201→ url_opt = stylesheet.contents(&guard).source_url.clone();\n   234→        assert_eq!(url_opt, test.1);\n   235→    }\n   236→}\n   237→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"86047ce8-ad0f-4b41-9642-961897412081","uuid":"f20f1421-fba8-48a0-888b-74c4f5e03363","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/tests/unit/style/stylesheets.rs","content":"/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n\nuse std::cell::RefCell;\n\nuse cssparser::SourceLocation;\nuse servo_arc::Arc;\nuse style::context::QuirksMode;\nuse style::error_reporting::{ContextualParseError, ParseErrorReporter};\nuse style::media_queries::MediaList;\nuse style::shared_lock::SharedRwLock;\nuse style::stylesheets::{\n    AllowImportRules, Origin, Stylesheet, StylesheetInDocument, UrlExtraData,\n};\nuse url::Url;\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct CSSError {\n    pub url: Arc<Url>,\n    pub line: u32,\n    pub column: u32,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\nstruct TestingErrorReporter {\n    errors: RefCell<Vec<CSSError>>,\n}\n\nimpl TestingErrorReporter {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        TestingErrorReporter {\n            errors: RefCell::new(Vec::new()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn assert_messages_contain(&self, expected_errors: &[(u32, u32, &str)]) {\n        let errors = self.errors.borrow();\n        for (i, (error, &(line, column, message))) in errors.iter().zip(expected_errors).enumerate()\n        {\n            assert_eq!(\n                (error.line, error.column),\n                (line, column),\n                \"line/column numbers of the {}th error: {:?}\",\n                i + 1,\n                error.message\n            );\n            assert!(\n                error.message.contains(message),\n                \"{:?} does not contain {:?}\",\n                error.message,\n                message\n            );\n        }\n        if errors.len() < expected_errors.len() {\n            panic!(\"Missing errors: {:#?}\", &expected_errors[errors.len()..]);\n        }\n        if errors.len() > expected_errors.len() {\n            panic!(\"Extra errors: {:#?}\", &errors[expected_errors.len()..]);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ParseErrorReporter for TestingErrorReporter {\n    fn report_error(\n        &self,\n        url: &UrlExtraData,\n        location: SourceLocation,\n        error: ContextualParseError,\n    ) {\n        self.errors.borrow_mut().push(CSSError {\n            url: url.0.clone(),\n            line: location.line,\n            column: location.column,\n            message: error.to_string(),\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_report_error_stylesheet() {\n    let css = r\"\n    div {\n        background-color: red;\n        display: invalid;\n        background-image: linear-gradient(0deg, black, invalid, transparent);\n        invalid: true;\n    }\n    @media (min-width: 10px invalid 1000px) {}\n    @font-face { src: url(), invalid, url(); }\n    @counter-style foo { symbols: a 0invalid b }\n    @font-feature-values Sans Sans { @foo {} @swash { foo: 1 invalid 2 } }\n    @invalid;\n    @media screen { @invalid; }\n    @supports (color: green) and invalid and (margin: 0) {}\n    @keyframes foo { from invalid {} to { margin: 0 invalid 0; } }\n    \";\n    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n\n    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n    Stylesheet::from_str(\n        css,\n        url.clone().into(),\n        Origin::UserAgent,\n        media,\n        lock,\n        None,\n        Some(&error_reporter),\n        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n        AllowImportRules::Yes,\n    );\n\n    error_reporter.assert_messages_contain(&[\n        (\n            3,\n            18,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'display: invalid;'\",\n        ),\n        (\n            4,\n            43,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'background-image:\",\n        ), // FIXME: column should be around 56\n        (5, 17, \"Unsupported property declaration: 'invalid: true;'\"),\n        (7, 28, \"Invalid media rule\"),\n        // When @counter-style is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n        (9, 19, \"Invalid rule: '@counter-style \"),\n        // When @font-feature-values is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n        (10, 25, \"Invalid rule: '@font-feature-values \"),\n        (11, 13, \"Invalid rule: '@invalid'\"),\n        (12, 29, \"Invalid rule: '@invalid'\"),\n        (13, 34, \"Invalid rule: '@supports \"),\n        (14, 26, \"Invalid keyframe rule: 'from invalid '\"),\n        (\n            14,\n            52,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'margin: 0 invalid 0;'\",\n        ),\n    ]);\n\n    assert_eq!(*error_reporter.errors.borrow()[0].url, url);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_report_unrecognized_vendor_properties() {\n    let css = r\"\n    div {\n        -o-background-color: red;\n        _background-color: red;\n        -moz-background-color: red;\n    }\n    \";\n    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n\n    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n    Stylesheet::from_str(\n        css,\n        url.into(),\n        Origin::UserAgent,\n        media,\n        lock,\n        None,\n        Some(&error_reporter),\n        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n        AllowImportRules::Yes,\n    );\n\n    error_reporter.assert_messages_contain(&[(\n        4,\n        31,\n        \"Unsupported property declaration: '-moz-background-color: red;'\",\n    )]);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_source_map_url() {\n    let tests = vec![\n        (\"\", None),\n        (\n            \"/*# sourceMappingURL=something */\",\n            Some(\"something\".to_string()),\n        ),\n    ];\n\n    for test in tests {\n        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n        let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n        let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n        let stylesheet = Stylesheet::from_str(\n            test.0,\n            url.into(),\n            Origin::UserAgent,\n            media,\n            lock.clone(),\n            None,\n            None,\n            QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n            AllowImportRules::Yes,\n        );\n        let guard = lock.read();\n        let url_opt = stylesheet.contents(&guard).source_map_url.clone();\n        assert_eq!(url_opt, test.1);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_source_url() {\n    let tests = vec![\n        (\"\", None),\n        (\"/*# sourceURL=something */\", Some(\"something\".to_string())),\n    ];\n\n    for test in tests {\n        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011VmHa1BP1Fx117m4ND24Jb","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→#![deny(unsafe_code)]\n     6→\n     7→//! Layout. Performs layout on the DOM, builds display lists and sends them to be\n     8→//! painted.\n     9→\n    10→mod cell;\n    11→mod context;\n    12→mod display_list;\n    13→mod dom;\n    14→mod dom_traversal;\n    15→mod flexbox;\n    16→pub mod flow;\n    17→mod formatting_contexts;\n    18→mod fragment_tree;\n    19→pub mod geom;\n    20→mod layout_box_base;\n    21→mod layout_impl;\n    22→mod taffy;\n    23→#[macro_use]\n    24→mod construct_modern;\n    25→mod lists;\n    26→mod positioned;\n    27→mod query;\n    28→mod quotes;\n    29→mod replaced;\n    30→mod sizing;\n    31→mod style_ext;\n    32→pub mod table;\n    33→mod traversal;\n    34→\n    35→use app_units::Au;\n    36→pub use cell::ArcRefCell;\n    37→pub(crate) use flow::BoxTree;\n    38→pub(crate) use fragment_tree::FragmentTree;\n    39→pub use layout_impl::LayoutFactoryImpl;\n    40→use malloc_size_of_derive::MallocSizeOf;\n    41→use servo_arc::Arc as ServoArc;\n    42→use style::logical_geometry::WritingMode;\n    43→use style::properties::ComputedValues;\n    44→\n    45→use crate::geom::LogicalVec2;\n    46→use crate::sizing::SizeConstraint;\n    47→use crate::style_ext::AspectRatio;\n    48→\n    49→/// At times, a style is \"owned\" by more than one layout object. For example, text\n    50→/// fragments need a handle on their parent inline box's style. In order to make\n    51→/// incremental layout easier to implement, another layer of shared ownership is added via\n    52→/// [`SharedStyle`]. This allows updating the style in originating layout object and\n    53→/// having all \"depdendent\" objects update automatically.\n    54→///\n    55→///  Note that this is not a cost-free data structure, so should only be\n    56→/// used when necessary.\n    57→pub(crate) type SharedStyle = ArcRefCell<ServoArc<ComputedValues>>;\n    58→\n    59→/// Represents the set of constraints that we use when computing the min-content\n    60→/// and max-content inline sizes of an element.\n    61→pub(crate) struct ConstraintSpace<'a> {\n    62→    pub block_size: SizeConstraint,\n    63→    pub style: &'a ComputedValues,\n    64→    pub preferred_aspect_ratio: Option<AspectRatio>,\n    65→}\n    66→\n    67→impl<'a> ConstraintSpace<'a> {\n    68→    fn new(\n    69→        block_size: SizeConstraint,\n    70→        style: &'a ComputedValues,\n    71→        preferred_aspect_ratio: Option<AspectRatio>,\n    72→    ) -> Self {\n    73→        Self {\n    74→            block_size,\n    75→            style,\n    76→            preferred_aspect_ratio,\n    77→        }\n    78→    }\n    79→\n    80→    fn new_for_style_and_ratio(\n    81→        style: &'a ComputedValues,\n    82→        preferred_aspect_ratio: Option<AspectRatio>,\n    83→    ) -> Self {\n    84→        Self::new(SizeConstraint::default(), style, preferred_aspect_ratio)\n    85→    }\n    86→}\n    87→\n    88→/// A variant of [`ContainingBlock`] that allows an indefinite inline size.\n    89→/// Useful for code that is shared for both layout (where we know the inline size\n    90→/// of the containing block) and intrinsic sizing (where we don't know it).\n    91→pub(crate) struct IndefiniteContainingBlock<'a> {\n    92→    pub size: LogicalVec2<Option<Au>>,\n    93→    pub style: &'a ComputedValues,\n    94→}\n    95→\n    96→impl<'a> From<&ConstraintSpace<'a>> for IndefiniteContainingBlock<'a> {\n    97→    fn from(constraint_space: &ConstraintSpace<'a>) -> Self {\n    98→        Self {\n    99→            size: LogicalVec2 {\n   100→                inline: None,\n   101→                block: constraint_space.block_size.to_definite(),\n   102→            },\n   103→            style: constraint_space.style,\n   104→        }\n   105→    }\n   106→}\n   107→\n   108→impl<'a> From<&'_ ContainingBlock<'a>> for IndefiniteContainingBlock<'a> {\n   109→    fn from(containing_block: &ContainingBlock<'a>) -> Self {\n   110→        Self {\n   111→            size: LogicalVec2 {\n   112→                inline: Some(containing_block.size.inline),\n   113→                block: containing_block.size.block.to_definite(),\n   114→            },\n   115→            style: containing_block.style,\n   116→        }\n   117→    }\n   118→}\n   119→\n   120→impl<'a> From<&'_ DefiniteContainingBlock<'a>> for IndefiniteContainingBlock<'a> {\n   121→    fn from(containing_block: &DefiniteContainingBlock<'a>) -> Self {\n   122→        Self {\n   123→            size: containing_block.size.map(|v| Some(*v)),\n   124→            style: containing_block.style,\n   125→        }\n   126→    }\n   127→}\n   128→\n   129→#[derive(Clone, Debug, MallocSizeOf)]\n   130→pub(crate) struct ContainingBlockSize {\n   131→    inline: Au,\n   132→    block: SizeConstraint,\n   133→}\n   134→\n   135→pub(crate) struct ContainingBlock<'a> {\n   136→    size: ContainingBlockSize,\n   137→    style: &'a ComputedValues,\n   138→}\n   139→\n   140→struct DefiniteContainingBlock<'a> {\n   141→    size: LogicalVec2<Au>,\n   142→    style: &'a ComputedValues,\n   143→}\n   144→\n   145→impl<'a> From<&'_ DefiniteContainingBlock<'a>> for ContainingBlock<'a> {\n   146→    fn from(definite: &DefiniteContainingBlock<'a>) -> Self {\n   147→        ContainingBlock {\n   148→            size: ContainingBlockSize {\n   149→                inline: definite.size.inline,\n   150→                block: SizeConstraint::Definite(definite.size.block),\n   151→            },\n   152→            style: definite.style,\n   153→        }\n   154→    }\n   155→}\n   156→\n   157→/// Data that is propagated from ancestors to descendants during [`crate::flow::BoxTree`]\n   158→/// construction.  This allows data to flow in the reverse direction of the typical layout\n   159→/// propoagation, but only during `BoxTree` construction.\n   160→#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]\n   161→struct PropagatedBoxTreeData {\n   162→    allow_percentage_column_in_tables: bool,\n   163→}\n   164→\n   165→impl Default for PropagatedBoxTreeData {\n   166→    fn default() -> Self {\n   167→        Self {\n   168→            allow_percentage_column_in_tables: true,\n   169→        }\n   170→    }\n   171→}\n   172→\n   173→impl PropagatedBoxTreeData {\n   174→    fn disallowing_percentage_table_columns(&self) -> PropagatedBoxTreeData {\n   175→        Self {\n   176→            allow_percentage_column_in_tables: false,\n   177→        }\n   178→    }\n   179→}\n   180→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. None,\n    52→    Contents,\n    53→    GeneratingBox(DisplayGeneratingBox),\n    54→}\n    55→\n    56→#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]\n    57→pub(crate) enum DisplayGeneratingBox {\n    58→    OutsideInside {\n    59→        outside: DisplayOutside,\n    60→        inside: DisplayInside,\n    61→    },\n    62→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-display-3/#layout-specific-display>\n    63→    LayoutInternal(DisplayLayoutInternal),\n    64→}\n    65→impl DisplayGeneratingBox {\n    66→    pub(crate) fn display_inside(&self) -> DisplayInside {\n    67→        match *self {\n    68→            DisplayGeneratingBox::OutsideInside { inside, .. } => inside,\n    69→            DisplayGeneratingBox::LayoutInternal(layout_internal) => {\n    70→                layout_internal.display_inside()\n    71→            },\n    72→        }\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    pub(crate) fn used_value_for_contents(&self, contents: &Contents) -> Self {\n    76→        // From <https://www.w3.org/TR/css-display-3/#layout-specific-display>:\n    77→        // > When the display property of a replaced element computes to one of\n    78→        // > the layout-internal values, it is handled as having a used value of\n    79→        // > inline.\n    80→        if matches!(self, Self::LayoutInternal(_)) && contents.is_replaced() {\n    81→            Self::OutsideInside {\n    82→                outside: DisplayOutside::Inline,\n    83→                inside: DisplayInside::Flow {\n    84→                    is_list_item: false,\n    85→                },\n    86→            }\n    87→        } else if matches!(contents, Contents::Widget(_)) {\n    88→            // If it's a widget, make sure the display-inside is flow-root.\n    89→            // <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#form-controls>\n    90→            // TODO: Do we want flow-root, or just an independent formatting context?\n    91→            if let DisplayGeneratingBox::OutsideInside { outside, .. } = self {\n    92→                DisplayGeneratingBox::OutsideInside {\n    93→                    outside: *outside,\n    94→                    inside: DisplayInside::FlowRoot {\n    95→                        is_list_item: false,\n    96→                    },\n    97→                }\n    98→            } else {\n    99→                *self\n   100→            }\n   101→        } else {\n   102→            *self\n   103→        }\n   104→    }\n   105→}\n   106→\n   107→#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]\n   108→pub(crate) enum DisplayOutside {\n   109→    Block,\n   110→    Inline,\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]\n   114→pub(crate) enum DisplayInside {\n   115→    // “list-items are limited to the Flow Layout display types”\n   116→    // <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-display/#list-items>\n   117→    Flow { is_list_item: bool },\n   118→    FlowRoot { is_list_item: bool },\n   119→    Flex,\n   120→    Grid,\n   121→    Table,\n   122→}\n   123→\n   124→#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]\n   125→#[expect(clippy::enum_variant_names)]\n   126→/// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-display-3/#layout-specific-display>\n   127→pub(crate) enum DisplayLayoutInternal {\n   128→    TableCaption,\n   129→    TableCell,\n   130→    TableColumn,\n   131→    TableColumnGroup,\n   132→    TableFooterGroup,\n   133→    TableHeaderGroup,\n   134→    TableRow,\n   135→    TableRowGroup,\n   136→}\n   137→\n   138→impl DisplayLayoutInternal {\n   139→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-display-3/#layout-specific-displa>\n   140→    pub(crate) fn display_inside(&self) TNode};\n    15→use style::selector_parser::RestyleDamage;\n    16→use style::traversal::{DomTraversal, PerLevelTraversalData, recalc_style_at};\n    17→use style::values::computed::Display;\n    18→\n    19→use crate::context::LayoutContext;\n    20→use crate::dom::{DOMLayoutData, NodeExt};\n    21→\n    22→pub struct RecalcStyle<'a> {\n    23→    context: &'a LayoutContext<'a>,\n    24→}\n    25→\n    26→impl<'a> RecalcStyle<'a> {\n    27→    pub(crate) fn new(context: &'a LayoutContext<'a>) -> Self {\n    28→        RecalcStyle { context }\n    29→    }\n    30→\n    31→    pub(crate) fn context(&self) -> &LayoutContext<'a> {\n    32→        self.context\n    33→    }\n    34→}\n    35→\n    36→#[expect(unsafe_code)]\n    37→impl<'dom, E> DomTraversal<E> for RecalcStyle<'_>\n    38→where\n    39→    E: TElement,\n    40→    E::ConcreteNode: 'dom + LayoutNode<'dom>,\n    41→{\n    42→    fn process_preorder<F>(\n    43→        &self,\n    44→        traversal_data: &PerLevelTraversalData,\n    45→        context: &mut StyleContext<E>,\n    46→        node: E::ConcreteNode,\n    47→        note_child: F,\n    48→    ) where\n    49→        F: FnMut(E::ConcreteNode),\n    50→    {\n    51→        if node.is_text_node() {\n    52→            return;\n    53→        }\n    54→\n    55→        let had_style_data = node.style_data().is_some();\n    56→        unsafe {\n    57→            node.initialize_style_and_layout_data::<DOMLayoutData>();\n    58→        }\n    59→\n    60→        let element = node.as_element().unwrap();\n    61→        let mut element_data = element.mutate_data().unwrap();\n    62→\n    63→        if !had_style_data {\n    64→            element_data.damage = RestyleDamage::reconstruct();\n    65→        }\n    66→\n    67→        recalc_style_at(\n    68→            self,\n    69→            traversal_data,\n    70→            context,\n    71→            element,\n    72→            &mut element_data,\n    73→            note_child,\n    74→        );\n    75→\n    76→        unsafe {\n    77→            element.unset_dirty_descendants();\n    78→        }\n    79→    }\n    80→\n    81→    #[inline]\n    82→    fn needs_postorder_traversal() -> bool {\n    83→        false\n    84→    }\n    85→\n    86→    fn process_postorder(&self, _style_context: &mut StyleContext<E>, _node: E::ConcreteNode) {\n    87→        panic!(\"this should never be called\")\n    88→    }\n    89→\n    90→    fn text_node_needs_traversal(node: E::ConcreteNode, parent_data: &ElementData) -> bool {\n    91→        node.layout_data().is_none() || !parent_data.damage.is_empty()\n    92→    }\n    93→\n    94→    fn shared_context(&self) -> &SharedStyleContext<'_> {\n    95→        &self.context.style_context\n    96→    }\n    97→}\n    98→\n    99→#[servo_tracing::instrument(skip_all)]\n   100→pub(crate) fn compute_damage_and_repair_style(\n   101→    context: &SharedStyleContext,\n   102→    node: ServoThreadSafeLayoutNode<'_>,\n   103→    damage_from_environment: RestyleDamage,\n   104→) -> RestyleDamage {\n   105→    compute_damage_and_repair_style_inner(context, node, damage_from_environment)\n   106→}\n   107→\n   108→pub(crate) fn compute_damage_and_repair_style_inner(\n   109→    context: &SharedStyleContext,\n   110→    node: ServoThreadSafeLayoutNode<'_>,\n   111→    damage_from_parent: RestyleDamage,\n   112→) -> RestyleDamage {\n   113→    let mut element_damage;\n   114→    let original_element_damage;\n   115→    let element_data = &node\n   116→        .style_data()\n   117→        .expect(\"Should not run `compute_damage` before styling.\")\n   118→        .element_data;\n   119→\n   120→    {\n   121→        let mut element_data = element_data.borrow_mut();\n   122→        original_element_damage = element_data.damage;\n   123→        element_damage = original_element_damage | damage_from_parent;\n   124→\n   125→        if let Some(ref style) = element_data.styles.primary {\n   126→            if style.get_box().display == Display::None {\n   127→                element_data.damage = element_damage;\n   128→                return element_damage;\n   129→            }\n   130→        }\n   131→    }\n   132→\n   133→    // If we are reconstructing this node, then all of the children should be reconstructed as well.\n   134→    // Otherwise, do not propagate down its box damage.\n   135→    let mut damage_for_children = element_damage;\n   136→    if !element_damage.contains(LayoutDamage::rebuild_box_tree()) {\n   137→        damage_for_children.truncate();\n   138→    }\n   139→\n   140→    let mut damage_from_children = RestyleDamage::empty();\n   141→    for child in node.children() {\n   142→        if child.is_element() {\n   143→            damage_from_children |=\n   144→                compute_damage_and_repair_style_inner(context, child, damage_for_children);\n   145→        }\n   146→    }\n   147→\n   148→    // If one of our children needed to be reconstructed, we need to recollect children\n   149→    // during box tree construction.\n   150→    if damage_from_children.contains(LayoutDamage::recollect_box_tree_children()) {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a         .as_ref()\n    77→            .and_then(|layout_box| layout_box.with_base(LayoutBoxBase::fragments))\n    78→            .unwrap_or_default()\n    79→    }\n    80→\n    81→    fn crate::layout_dom::{ServoLayoutNode, ServoShadowRoot, ServoThreadSafeLayoutNode};\n    57→\n    58→/// A wrapper around elements that ensures layout can only ever access safe properties.\n    59→#[derive(Clone, Copy, Eq, Hash, PartialEq)]\n    60→pub struct ServoLayoutElement<'dom> {\n    61→    /// The wrapped private DOM Element.\n    62→    element: LayoutDom<'dom, Element>,\n    63→}\n    64→\n    65→impl fmt::Debug for ServoLayoutElement<'_> {\n    66→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter) -> fmt::Result {\n    67→        write!(f, \"<{}\", self.element.local_name())?;\n    68→        if let Some(id) = self.id() {\n    69→            write!(f, \" id={}\", id)?;\n    70→        }\n    71→        write!(f, \"> ({:#x})\", self.as_node().opaque().0)\n    72→    }\n    73→}\n    74→\n    75→impl<'dom> ServoLayoutElement<'dom> {\n    76→    pub(super) fn from_layout_js(el: LayoutDom<'dom, Element>) -> Self {\n    77→        ServoLayoutElement { element: el }\n    78→    }\n    79→\n    80→    pub(super) fn is_html_element(&self) -> bool {\n    81→        self.element.is_html_element()\n    82→    }\n    83→\n    84→    #[inline]\n    85→    fn get_attr_enum(&self, namespace: &Namespace, name: &LocalName) -> Option<&AttrValue> {\n    86→        self.element.get_attr_for_layout(namespace, name)\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    #[inline]\n    90→    fn get_attr(&self, namespace: &Namespace, name: &LocalName) -> Option<&str> {\n    91→        self.element.get_attr_val_for_layout(namespace, name)\n    92→    }\n    93→\n    94→    fn get_style_data(&self) -> Option<&StyleData> {\n    95→        self.as_node().style_data()\n    96→    }\n    97→\n    98→    /// Unset the snapshot flags on the underlying DOM object for this element.\n    99→    ///\n   100→    /// # Safety\n   101→    ///\n   102→    /// This function accesses and modifies the underlying DOM object and should\n   103→    /// not be used by more than a single thread at once.\n   104→    pub unsafe fn unset_snapshot_flags(&self) {\n   105→        unsafe {\n   106→            self.as_node()\n   107→                .node\n   108→                .set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_SNAPSHOT | NodeFlags::HANDLED_SNAPSHOT, false);\n   109→        }\n   110→    }\n   111→\n   112→    /// Unset the snapshot flags on the underlying DOM object for this element.\n   113→    ///\n   114→    /// # Safety\n   115→    ///\n   116→    /// This function accesses and modifies the underlying DOM object and should\n   117→    /// not be used by more than a single thread at once.\n   118→    pub unsafe fn set_has_snapshot(&self) {\n   119→        unsafe {\n   120→            self.as_node().node.set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_SNAPSHOT, true);\n   121→        }\n   122→    }\n   123→\n   124→    /// Returns true if this element is the body child of an html element root element.\n   125→    fn is_body_element_of_html_element_root(&self) -> bool {\n   126→        if self.element.local_name() != &local_name!(\"body\") {\n   127→            return false;\n   128→        }\n   129→\n   130→        self.parent_element().is_some_and(|element| {\n   131→            element.is_root() && element.element.local_name() == &local_name!(\"html\")\n   132→        })\n   133→    }\n   134→\n   135→    /// Returns the parent element of this element, if it has one.\n   136→    fn parent_element(&self) -> Option<Self> {\n   137→        self.as_node().parent_element()\n   138→    }\n   139→\n   140→    fn is_root(&self) -> bool {\n   141→        match self.as_node().parent_node() {\n   142→            None => false,\n   143→            Some(node) => matches!(node.script_type_id(), NodeTypeId::Document(_)),\n   144→        }\n   145→    }\n   146→}\n   147→\n   148→pub enum DOMDescendantIterator<E>\n   149→where\n   150→    E: TElement,\n   151→{\n   152→    /// Iterating over the children of a node, including children of a potential\n   153→    /// [ShadowRoot](crate::dom::shadow_root::ShadowRoot)\n   154→    Children(DomChildren<E::ConcreteNode>),\n   155→    /// Iterating over the content's of a [`<slot>`](HTMLSlotElement) element.\n   156→    Slottables { slot: E, index: usize },\n   157→}\n   158→\n   159→impl<E> Iterator for DOMDescendantIterator<E>\n   160→where\n   161→    E: TElement,\n   162→{\n   163→    type Item = E::ConcreteNode;\n   164→\n   165→    fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item> {\n   166→        match self {\n   167→            Self::Children(children) => children.next(),\n   168→   172→                Some(*slot)\n   173→            },\n   174→        }\n   175→    }\n   176→}\n   177→\n   178→impl<'dom> style::dom::AttributeProvider for ServoLayoutElement<'dom> {\n   179→ This implements the `LayoutNode` trait as well as\n    40→/// several style and selectors traits for use during layout. This version\n    41→/// should only be used on a single thread. 67→        }\n    68→    }\n    69→}\n    70→\n    71→impl<'dom> ServoLayoutNode<'dom> {\n    72→    pub(super) fn from_layout_js(n: LayoutDom<'dom, Node>) -> Self {\n    73→        ServoLayoutNode {    // Mutate the declaration block associated to this style owner, and\n    63→    // optionally indicate if it has changed (assumed to be true).\n    64→    fn mutate_associated_block<F, R>(&self, f: F, can_gc: CanGc) -> R\n    65→    where\n    66→        F: FnOnce(&mut PropertyDeclarationBlock, &mut bool) -> R,\n    67→    {\n    68→        // TODO(emilio): This has some duplication just to avoid dummy clones.\n    69→        //\n    70→        // This is somewhat complex but the complexity is encapsulated.\n    71→        let mut changed = true;\n    72→        match *self {\n    73→            CSSStyleOwner::Null => unreachable!(\n    74→                \"CSSStyleDeclaration should always be read-only when CSSStyleOwner is Null\"\n    75→            ),\n    76→            CSSStyleOwner::Element(ref el) => {\n    77→                let document = el.owner_document();\n    78→                let shared_lock = document.style_shared_lock();\n    79→                let mut attr = el.style_attribute().borrow_mut().take();\n    80→                let result = if let Some(lock) = attr.as_ref() {\n    81→                    let mut guard = shared_lock.write();\n    82→                    let pdb = lock.write_with(&mut guard);\n    83→                    f(pdb, &mut changed)\n    84→                } else {\n    85→                    let mut pdb = PropertyDeclarationBlock::new();\n    86→                    let result = f(&mut pdb, &mut changed);\n    87→\n    88→                    // Here `changed` is somewhat silly, because we know the\n    89→                    // exact conditions under it changes.\n    90→                    changed = !pdb.declarations().is_empty();\n    91→                    if changed {\n    92→                        attr = Some(Arc::new(shared_lock.wrap(pdb)));\n    93→                    }\n    94→\n    95→                    result\n    96→                };\n    97→\n    98→                if changed {\n    99→                    // Note that there's no need to remove the attribute here if\n   100→                    // the declaration block is empty[1], and if `attr` is\n   101→                    // `None` it means that it necessarily didn't change, so no\n   102→                    // need to go through all the set_attribute machinery.\n   103→                    //\n   104→                    // [1]: https://github.com/whatwg/html/issues/2306\n   105→                    if let Some(pdb) = attr {\n   106→                        let guard = shared_lock.read();\n   107→                &local_name!(\"style\"),\n   111→                            AttrValue::Declaration(serialization, pdb),\n   112→                            can_gc,\n   113→                        );\n   114→                    }\n   115→                } else {\n   116→                    // Remember to put it back.\n   117→                    *el.style_attribute().borrow_mut() = attr;\n   118→                }\n   119→\n   120→                result\n   121→            },\n   122→            CSSStyleOwner::CSSRule(ref rule, ref pdb) => {\n   123→ 130→                }\n   131→                result\n   132→            },\n   133→        }\n   134→    }\n   135→\n   136→    fn with_block<F, R>(&self, f: F) -> R\n   137→    where\n   138→ Let me compile a comprehensive report on how stylo is used in Servo.\n\n## Stylo Usage in Servo - Comprehensive Analysis\n\nBased on my search of the Servo codebase in the components/ directory, here's a detailed understanding of how stylo is integrated:\n\n### 1. WHERE STYLE/STYLO IS IMPORTED\n\n**Main integration points:**\n\n- **Layout Component** (`/workspace/components/layout/`):\n  - Imports `stylo`, `stylo_atoms`, and `stylo_traits` in `Cargo.toml`\n  - Heavy use in `/workspace/components/layout/style_ext.rs` for style-related extensions\n  - Used in `/workspace/components/layout/traversal.rs` for style recalculation\n  - Used in `/workspace/components/layout/taffy/stylo_taffy/` for layout algorithm integration\n\n- **Script Component** (`/workspace/components/script/`):\n  - Imports `stylo`, `stylo_atoms`, `stylo_dom`, `stylo_malloc_size_of`, and `stylo_traits` in `Cargo.toml`\n  - Heavy use throughout DOM implementation\n  - Used in layout_dom wrappers for layout-safe access to DOM\n  - Used in CSS implementation files\n\n**Key import patterns:**\n```rust\nuse style::properties::ComputedValues\nuse style::properties::{PropertyDeclaration, PropertyDeclarationBlock}\nuse style::properties::style_structs::*  // (Font, Border, etc.)\nuse style::computed_values::*\nuse style::values::computed::*\nuse style::selector_parser::{PseudoElement, RestyleDamage}\nuse style::context::{SharedStyleContext, StyleContext}\nuse style::traversal::{DomTraversal, recalc_style_at}\nuse style::stylesheets::{Stylesheet, StylesheetInDocument}\nuse stylo_atoms::Atom\nuse stylo_dom::ElementState\n```\n\n### 2. LOCAL STYLE CODE EXTENDING STYLO\n\n**Primary extension point: `/workspace/components/layout/style_ext.rs`**\n\nThis file implements a **`ComputedValuesExt` trait** that extends stylo's `ComputedValues`:\n- Accessor methods like `get_position()`, `get_box()`, `get_margin()`, `get_padding()`, `get_border()`, `get_background()`, `get_text()`, `get_inherited_box()`, `get_inherited_text()`, `get_column()`, `get_effects()`, `get_svg()`\n- Conversion methods for layout-specific types (e.g., `physical_box_offsets()`, `box_size()`, `content_box_size_for_box_size()`)\n- Layout-specific calculations and helpers\n\n**Helper trait definition structure:**\n```rust\npub(crate) trait ComputedValuesExt {\n    fn physical_box_offsets(&self) -> PhysicalSides<LengthPercentageOrAuto<'_>>;\n    fn box_size(&self, containing_block_writing_mode: WritingMode) -> LogicalVec2<Size<LengthPercentage>>;\n    fn get_position(&self) -> &'_ style::properties::style_structs::Position;\n    fn get_box(&self) -> &'_ style::properties::style_structs::Box;\n    // ... many more accessors\n}\n\nimpl ComputedValuesExt for ComputedValues { /* implementations */ }\n```\n\n### 3. HOW PROPERTIES LIKE CONTAINER-TYPE WOULD BE DEFINED\n\n**Property definition approach in stylo:**\n\nProperties are defined in **style structs**, which are generated from longhand property definitions in stylo. For a property like `container-type`:\n\n1. **Definition**: Would be in a `style_structs` module within stylo itself (not in Servo's components)\n2. **Access Pattern**: In Servo, you'd access it via:\n   ```rust\n   computed_values.get_box().container_type  // If it's part of the box struct\n   // or\n   computed_values.container_type            // If directly on ComputedValues\n   ```\n\n3. **Parsing**: Handled by `/workspace/components/script/css.rs` and stylesheet loading in `/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_loader.rs`\n\n**Property Declaration Blocks** are used in:\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstyledeclaration.rs` - for inline style handling\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/element.rs` - for element style attributes\n- Uses `PropertyDeclarationBlock` and `PropertyDeclaration` from stylo\n\n### 4. WHERE CSS PARSING HAPPENS\n\n**CSS Parsing Flow:**\n\n1. **Stylesheet Loading** (`/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_loader.rs`):\n   - `StylesheetLoader` trait implementation\n   - Handles external stylesheet fetching and parsing\n   - Uses `style::stylesheets::Stylesheet` for parsing\n\n2. **Stylesheet Processing** (`/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_set.rs`):\n   - `StylesheetSetRef` enum manages `AuthorStylesheetSet` and `DocumentStylesheetSet`\n   - Orchestrates stylesheet addition/removal\n\n3. **DOM CSS Rules** (`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/`):\n   - `cssstylesheet.rs` - Wraps stylo's `Stylesheet` (line 61: `style_stylesheet: DomRefCell<Arc<StyleStyleSheet>>`)\n   - `cssstyledeclaration.rs` - Handles inline style parsing via `parse_style_attribute()` from stylo\n   - Uses `Parser` and `ParserContext` from stylo\n\n4. **Inline Styles**:\n   - Parsed in `/workspace/components/script/css.rs` via `parser_context_for_document()`\n   - Creates `ParserContext` with `Origin::Author`, quirks mode, error reporting\n\n### 5. WHERE STYLE MATCHING/CASCADE HAPPENS\n\n**Style Resolution Flow:**\n\n1. **Main Stylist** (`/workspace/components/layout/layout_impl.rs`):\n   - Line 133: `stylist: Stylist` - the core style matching engine from stylo\n   - Initialized with user-agent, author, and other stylesheets\n\n2. **Style Recalculation Traversal** (`/workspace/components/layout/traversal.rs`):\n   - `RecalcStyle` struct implements `DomTraversal<E>` trait from stylo\n   - `process_preorder()` method handles pre-order traversal\n   - Calls `recalc_style_at()` from stylo to compute styles for each element\n   - Computes `RestyleDamage` to determine layout impact\n\n3. **DOM Node Integration** (`/workspace/components/script/layout_dom/`):\n   - `ServoLayoutNode<'dom>` implements `style::dom::TNode` trait\n   - `ServoLayoutElement<'dom>` implements `style::dom::TElement` trait\n   - These traits bridge DOM structure with stylo's selector matching\n   - Methods: `as_node()`, `traversal_children()`, `parent_node()`, `style_data()`, etc.\n\n4. **Element Data Storage**:\n   - `/workspace/components/layout/dom.rs` - `InnerDOMLayoutData` stores layout information\n   - Style data stored via `ElementData` from stylo\n   - Damage computation in `traversal.rs` via `compute_damage_and_repair_style()`\n\n5. **Style Context** (`/workspace/components/layout/context.rs`):\n   - `LayoutContext` contains `SharedStyleContext` from stylo\n   - Provides shared style computation context to layout engine\n\n### Key Files Summary:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `/workspace/components/layout/style_ext.rs` | ComputedValues extensions for layout |\n| `/workspace/components/layout/traversal.rs` | Style recalculation and damage computation |\n| `/workspace/components/layout/taffy/stylo_taffy/` | Stylo-to-Taffy layout algorithm integration |\n| `/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_loader.rs` | External stylesheet loading |\n| `/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_set.rs` | Stylesheet collection management |\n| `/workspace/components/script/layout_dom/element.rs` | DOM-to-Stylo TElement trait impl |\n| `/workspace/components/script/layout_dom/node.rs` | DOM-to-Stylo TNode trait impl |\n| `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstylesheet.rs` | CSS stylesheet DOM wrapper |\n| `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstyledeclaration.rs` | Inline style parsing & access |\n| `/workspace/components/script/css.rs` | CSS parsing context helpers |\n| `/workspace/components/layout/layout_impl.rs` | Main Stylist initialization |\n\nThe architecture shows Servo's clean separation where **stylo handles all CSS parsing, selector matching, and style computation**, while Servo's components layer provides DOM integration and layout-specific extensions on top of stylo's computed values."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a185e38deea696081 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 66210\ntool_uses: 42\nduration_ms: 55152</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"86047ce8-ad0f-4b41-9642-961897412081","uuid":"9372bbbb-0171-470a-a111-81ce3413beb8","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Search the Servo codebase for how stylo (the style crate) is used. Let me compile a comprehensive report on how stylo is used in Servo.\n\n## Stylo Usage in Servo - Comprehensive Analysis\n\nBased on my search of the Servo codebase in the components/ directory, here's a detailed understanding of how stylo is integrated:\n\n### 1. WHERE STYLE/STYLO IS IMPORTED\n\n**Main integration points:**\n\n- **Layout Component** (`/workspace/components/layout/`):\n  - Imports `stylo`, `stylo_atoms`, and `stylo_traits` in `Cargo.toml`\n  - Heavy use in `/workspace/components/layout/style_ext.rs` for style-related extensions\n  - Used in `/workspace/components/layout/traversal.rs` for style recalculation\n  - Used in `/workspace/components/layout/taffy/stylo_taffy/` for layout algorithm integration\n\n- **Script Component** (`/workspace/components/script/`):\n  - Imports `stylo`, `stylo_atoms`, `stylo_dom`, `stylo_malloc_size_of`, and `stylo_traits` in `Cargo.toml`\n  - Heavy use throughout DOM implementation\n  - Used in layout_dom wrappers for layout-safe access to DOM\n  - Used in CSS implementation files\n\n**Key import patterns:**\n```rust\nuse style::properties::ComputedValues\nuse style::properties::{PropertyDeclaration, PropertyDeclarationBlock}\nuse style::properties::style_structs::*  // (Font, Border, etc.)\nuse style::computed_values::*\nuse style::values::computed::*\nuse style::selector_parser::{PseudoElement, RestyleDamage}\nuse style::context::{SharedStyleContext, StyleContext}\nuse style::traversal::{DomTraversal, recalc_style_at}\nuse style::stylesheets::{Stylesheet, StylesheetInDocument}\nuse stylo_atoms::Atom\nuse stylo_dom::ElementState\n```\n\n### 2. LOCAL STYLE CODE EXTENDING STYLO\n\n**Primary extension point: `/workspace/components/layout/style_ext.rs`**\n\nThis file implements a **`ComputedValuesExt` trait** that extends stylo's `ComputedValues`:\n- Accessor methods like `get_position()`, `get_box()`, `get_margin()`, `get_padding()`, `get_border()`, `get_background()`, `get_text()`, `get_inherited_box()`, `get_inherited_text()`, `get_column()`, `get_effects()`, `get_svg()`\n- Conversion methods for layout-specific types (e.g., `physical_box_offsets()`, `box_size()`, `content_box_size_for_box_size()`)\n- Layout-specific calculations and helpers\n\n**Helper trait definition structure:**\n```rust\npub(crate) trait ComputedValuesExt {\n    fn physical_box_offsets(&self) -> PhysicalSides<LengthPercentageOrAuto<'_>>;\n    fn box_size(&self, containing_block_writing_mode: WritingMode) -> LogicalVec2<Size<LengthPercentage>>;\n    fn get_position(&self) -> &'_ style::properties::style_structs::Position;\n    fn get_box(&self) -> &'_ style::properties::style_structs::Box;\n    // ... many more accessors\n}\n\nimpl ComputedValuesExt for ComputedValues { /* implementations */ }\n```\n\n### 3. HOW PROPERTIES LIKE CONTAINER-TYPE WOULD BE DEFINED\n\n**Property definition approach in stylo:**\n\nProperties are defined in **style structs**, which are generated from longhand property definitions in stylo. For a property like `container-type`:\n\n1. **Definition**: Would be in a `style_structs` module within stylo itself (not in Servo's components)\n2. **Access Pattern**: In Servo, you'd access it via:\n   ```rust\n   computed_values.get_box().container_type  // If it's part of the box struct\n   // or\n   computed_values.container_type            // If directly on ComputedValues\n   ```\n\n3. **Parsing**: Handled by `/workspace/components/script/css.rs` and stylesheet loading in `/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_loader.rs`\n\n**Property Declaration Blocks** are used in:\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstyledeclaration.rs` - for inline style handling\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/element.rs` - for element style attributes\n- Uses `PropertyDeclarationBlock` and `PropertyDeclaration` from stylo\n\n### 4. WHERE CSS PARSING HAPPENS\n\n**CSS Parsing Flow:**\n\n1. **Stylesheet Loading** (`/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_loader.rs`):\n   - `StylesheetLoader` trait implementation\n   - Handles external stylesheet fetching and parsing\n   - Uses `style::stylesheets::Stylesheet` for parsing\n\n2. **Stylesheet Processing** (`/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_set.rs`):\n   - `StylesheetSetRef` enum manages `AuthorStylesheetSet` and `DocumentStylesheetSet`\n   - Orchestrates stylesheet addition/removal\n\n3. **DOM CSS Rules** (`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/`):\n   - `cssstylesheet.rs` - Wraps stylo's `Stylesheet` (line 61: `style_stylesheet: DomRefCell<Arc<StyleStyleSheet>>`)\n   - `cssstyledeclaration.rs` - Handles inline style parsing via `parse_style_attribute()` from stylo\n   - Uses `Parser` and `ParserContext` from stylo\n\n4. **Inline Styles**:\n   - Parsed in `/workspace/components/script/css.rs` via `parser_context_for_document()`\n   - Creates `ParserContext` with `Origin::Author`, quirks mode, error reporting\n\n### 5. WHERE STYLE MATCHING/CASCADE HAPPENS\n\n**Style Resolution Flow:**\n\n1. **Main Stylist** (`/workspace/components/layout/layout_impl.rs`):\n   - Line 133: `stylist: Stylist` - the core style matching engine from stylo\n   - Initialized with user-agent, author, and other stylesheets\n\n2. **Style Recalculation Traversal** (`/workspace/components/layout/traversal.rs`):\n   - `RecalcStyle` struct implements `DomTraversal<E>` trait from stylo\n   - `process_preorder()` method handles pre-order traversal\n   - Calls `recalc_style_at()` from stylo to compute styles for each element\n   - Computes `RestyleDamage` to determine layout impact\n\n3. **DOM Node Integration** (`/workspace/components/script/layout_dom/`):\n   - `ServoLayoutNode<'dom>` implements `style::dom::TNode` trait\n   - `ServoLayoutElement<'dom>` implements `style::dom::TElement` trait\n   - These traits bridge DOM structure with stylo's selector matching\n   - Methods: `as_node()`, `traversal_children()`, `parent_node()`, `style_data()`, etc.\n\n4. **Element Data Storage**:\n   - `/workspace/components/layout/dom.rs` - `InnerDOMLayoutData` stores layout information\n   - Style data stored via `ElementData` from stylo\n   - Damage computation in `traversal.rs` via `compute_damage_and_repair_style()`\n\n5. **Style Context** (`/workspace/components/layout/context.rs`):\n   - `LayoutContext` contains `SharedStyleContext` from stylo\n   - Provides shared style computation context to layout engine\n\n### Key Files Summary:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `/workspace/components/layout/style_ext.rs` | ComputedValues extensions for layout |\n| `/workspace/components/layout/traversal.rs` | Style recalculation and damage computation |\n| `/workspace/components/layout/taffy/stylo_taffy/` | Stylo-to-Taffy layout algorithm integration |\n| `/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_loader.rs` | External stylesheet loading |\n| `/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_set.rs` | Stylesheet collection management |\n| `/workspace/components/script/layout_dom/element.rs` | DOM-to-Stylo TElement trait impl |\n| `/workspace/components/script/layout_dom/node.rs` | DOM-to-Stylo TNode trait impl |\n| `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstylesheet.rs` | CSS stylesheet DOM wrapper |\n| `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstyledeclaration.rs` | Inline style parsing & access |\n| `/workspace/components/script/css.rs` | CSS parsing context helpers |\n| `/workspace/components/layout/layout_impl.rs` | Main Stylist initialization |\n\nThe architecture shows Servo's clean separation where **stylo handles all CSS parsing, selector matching, and style computation**, while Servo's components layer provides DOM integration and layout-specific extensions on top of stylo's computed values."}],"totalDurationMs":55152,"totalTokens":66210,"totalToolUseCount":42,"usage":{"input_tokens":1734,"cache_creation_input_tokens":418,"cache_read_input_tokens":61845,"output_tokens":2213,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":418,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

     MediaQueryListMatchState::Changed\n    72→            }\n    73→        } else {\n    74→            MediaQueryListMatchState::Changed\n    75→        };\n    76→\n    77→ &SharedRwLock {\n    44→        self.cssgroupingrule.shared_lock()\n    45→    }\n    46→\n    47→    pub(crate) fn clone_rules(&self) -> Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>> {\n    48→        self.rules.borrow().clone()\n    49→    }\n    50→\n    51→    pub(crate) fn update_rules(\n    52→        &self,\n    53→        rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>,\n    54→        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    55→    ) {\n    56→        self.cssgroupingrule.update_rules(&rules, guard);\n    57→        *self.rules.borrow_mut() = rules;\n    58→    }\n    59→}\n    60→\n    61→impl CSSConditionRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSConditionRule {\n    62→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#dom-cssconditionrule-conditiontext>\n    63→    fn ConditionText(&self) -> DOMString {\n    64→        if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n    65→            rule.get_condition_text()\n    66→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n    67→            rule.get_condition_text()\n    68→        } else {\n    69→            unreachable!()\n    70→        }\n    71→    }\n    72→}\n <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#parse-a-media-query-list>\n    67→    pub(crate) fn parse_media_list(value: &str, window: &Window) -> StyleMediaList {\n    68→        if value.is_empty() {\n    69→            return StyleMediaList::empty();\n    70→        }\n    71→        let mut input = ParserInput::new(value);\n    72→        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    73→        let document = window.Document();\n    74→        let url_data = UrlExtraData(document.owner_global().api_base_url().get_arc());\n    75→        // FIXME(emilio): This looks somewhat fishy, since we use the context\n    76→        // only to parse the media query list, CssRuleType::Media doesn't make\n    77→        // much sense.\n    78→        let context = parser_context_for_document(\n    79→            &document,\n    80→            CssRuleType::Media,\n    81→            ParsingMode::DEFAULT,\n    82→            &url_data,\n    83→        );\n    84→        StyleMediaList::parse(&context, &mut parser)\n    85→    }\n    86→\n    87→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#parse-a-media-query>\n    88→    pub(crate) fn parse_media_query<'i>(\n    89→        value: &'i str,\n    90→        window: &Window,\n    91→    ) -> Result<MediaQuery, ParseError<'i>> {\n    92→        let mut input = ParserInput::new(value);\n    93→        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    94→ self.media_queries.borrow().read_with(&guard).clone()\n   108→    }\n   109→\n   110→    pub(crate) fn update_media_list(&self, media_queries: Arc<Locked<StyleMediaList>>) {\n   111→        *self.media_queries.borrow_mut() = media_queries;\n   112→    }\n   113→\n   114→    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#matches-the-environment>\n   115→    pub(crate) fn matches_environment(document: &Document, media_query: &str) -> bool {\n   116→        let quirks_mode = document.quirks_mode();\n   117→        let url_data = UrlExtraData(document.owner_global().api_base_url().get_arc());\n   118→        // FIXME(emilio): This should do the same that we do for other media\n   119→        // lists regarding the rule type and such, though it doesn't really\n   120→        // matter right now...\n   121→        //\n   122→        // Also, ParsingMode::all() is wrong, and should be DEFAULT.\n   123→        let context = ParserContext::new(\n   124→            Origin::Author,\n   125→            &url_data,\n   126→            Some(CssRuleType::Style),\n   127→            ParsingMode::all(),\n   128→  142→}\n   143→\n   144→impl MediaListMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for MediaList {\n   145→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-mediatext>\n   146→    fn MediaText(&self) -> SetMediaText(&self, value: DOMString) {\n   158→        self.parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   159→        let global = self.global();\n   160→        let mut guard = self.shared_lock().write();\n   161→        let media_queries_borrowed = self.media_queries.borrow();\n   162→        let media_queries = media_queries_borrowed.write_with(&mut guard);\n   163→        *media_queries = Self::parse_media_list(&value.str(), global.as_window());\n   164→        self.parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   165→    }\n   166→\n   167→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-length>\n   168→    fn Length(&self) -> u32 {\n   169→        let guard = self.shared_lock().read();\n   170→        self.media_queries\n   171→ <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-item>\n   178→    fn Item(&self, index: u32) -> Option<DOMString> {\n   179→        let guard = self.shared_lock().read();\n   180→        self.media_queries\n   181→            .borrow()\n   182→            .read_with(&guard)\n   183→            .media_queries\n   184→            .get(index as usize)\n   185→            .map(|query| 190→        self.Item(index)\n   191→    }\n   192→\n   193→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-appendmedium>\n   194→    fn AppendMedium(&self, medium: DOMString) {\n   195→        // Step 1\n   196→        let global = self.global();\n   197→        let medium = medium.str();\n   198→        let m = Self::parse_media_query(&medium, global.as_window());\n   199→        // Step 2\n   200→        if m.is_err() {\n   201→            return;\n   202→        }\n   203→        // Step 3\n   204→        {\n   205→            let m_serialized = m.clone().unwrap().to_css_string();\n   206→            let guard = self.shared_lock().read();\n   207→            let any = self\n   208→                .media_queries\n   209→                .borrow()\n   210→                .read_with(&guard)\n   211→                .media_queries\n   212→                .iter()\n   213→                .any(|q| m_serialized == q.to_css_string());\n   214→            if any {\n   215→                return;\n   216→            }\n   217→        }\n   218→        // Step 4\n   219→        self.parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   220→        let mut guard = self.shared_lock().write();\n   221→        self.media_queries\n   222→            .borrow()\n   223→            .write_with(&mut guard)\n   224→            .media_queries\n   225→            .push(m.unwrap());\n   226→        self.parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   227→    }\n   228→\n   229→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-deletemedium>\n   230→    fn DeleteMedium(&self, medium: DOMString) {\n   231→        // Step 1\n   232→        let global = self.global();\n   233→        let medium = medium.str();\n   234→        let m = Self::parse_media_query(&medium, global.as_window());\n   235→        // Step 2\n   236→        if m.is_err() {\n   237→            return;\n   238→        }\n   239→        // Step 3\n   240→        self.parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   241→        let media_list = media_queries_borrowed.write_with(&mut guard);\n   245→        let new_vec = media_list\n   246→            .media_queries\n   247→            .drain(..)\n   248→            .filter(|q| m_serialized != q.to_css_string())\n   249→            .collect();\n   250→        media_list.media_queries = new_vec;\n   251→        self.parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   252→    }\n   32→unsafe_no_jsmanaged_fields!(RulesSource);\n    33→\n    34→impl Convert<Error> for RulesMutateError {\n    35→    fn convert(self) -> Error {\n    36→        match self {\n    37→            RulesMutateError::Syntax => Error::Syntax(None),\n    38→            RulesMutateError::IndexSize => Error::IndexSize(None),\n    39→            RulesMutateError::HierarchyRequest => Error::HierarchyRequest(None),\n    40→            RulesMutateError::InvalidState => Error::InvalidState(None),\n    41→        }\n    42→    }\n    43→}\n    44→\n    45→#[dom_struct]\n    46→pub(crate) struct CSSRuleList {\n    47→    reflector_: Reflector,\n    48→    parent_stylesheet: Dom<CSSStyleSheet>,\n    49→    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Stylo\"]\n    50→    rules: RefCell<RulesSource>,\n    51→    dom_rules: DomRefCell<Vec<MutNullableDom<CSSRule>>>,\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→pub(crate) enum RulesSource {\n    55→    Rules(Arc<Locked<CssRules>>),\n    56→    Keyframes(Arc<Locked<KeyframesRule>>),\n    57→}\n    58→\n    59→impl CSSRuleList {\n    60→    #[cfg_attr(crown, expect(crown::unrooted_must_root))]\n    61→    pub(crate) fn new_inherited(\n    62→        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    63→        rules: RulesSource,\n    64→    ) -> CSSRuleList {\n    65→        let guard = parent_stylesheet.shared_lock().read();\n    66→        let dom_rules = match rules {\n    67→            RulesSource::Rules(ref rules) => rules\n    68→                .read_with(&guard)\n    69→                .0\n    70→                .iter()\n    71→                .map(|_| MutNullableDom::new(None))\n    72→                .collect(),\n    73→            RulesSource::Keyframes(ref rules) => rules\n    74→                .read_with(&guard)\n    75→                .keyframes\n    76→                .iter()\n    77→                .map(|_| MutNullableDom::new(None))\n    78→                .collect(),\n    79→        };\n    80→\n    81→        CSSRuleList {\n    82→            reflector_: Reflector::new(),\n    83→            parent_stylesheet: Dom::from_ref(parent_stylesheet),\n    84→            rules: RefCell::new(rules),\n    85→            dom_rules: DomRefCell::new(dom_rules),\n    86→        }\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    pub(crate) fn new(\n    90→        window: &Window,\n    91→        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    92→        rules: RulesSource,\n    93→        can_gc: CanGc,\n    94→    ) -> DomRoot<CSSRuleList> {\n    95→        reflect_dom_object(\n    96→            Box::new(CSSRuleList::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, rules)),\n    97→            window,\n    98→            can_gc,\n    99→        )\n   100→    }\n   101→\n   102→    /// Should only be called for CssRules-backed rules. Use append_lazy_rule\n   103→    /// for keyframes-backed rules.\n   104→    pub(crate) fn insert_rule(\n   105→        &self,\n   106→        rule: &DOMString,\n   107→        idx: u32,\n   108→        containing_rule_types: CssRuleTypes,\n   109→        parse_relative_rule_type: Option<CssRuleType>,\n   110→        can_gc: CanGc,\n   111→    ) -> Fallible<u32> {\n   112→        self.parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   113→        let css_rules = if let RulesSource::Rules(rules) = &*self.rules.borrow() {\n   114→            rules.clone()\n   115→        } else {\n   116→            panic!(\"Called insert_rule on non-CssRule-backed CSSRuleList\");\n   117→        };\n   118→\n   119→        let global = self.global();\n   120→        let window = global.as_window();\n   121→        let index = idx as usize;\n   122→\n   123→        let parent_stylesheet = self.parent_stylesheet.style_stylesheet();\n   124→        let owner = self\n   125→            .parent_stylesheet\n   126→            .owner_node()\n   127→            .and_then(DomRoot::downcast::<HTMLElement>);\n   128→        let loader = owner\n   129→            .as_ref()\n   130→            .map(|element| ElementStylesheetLoader::new(element));\n   131→        let allow_import_rules = if self.parent_stylesheet.is_constructed() {\n   132→            AllowImportRules::No\n   133→        } else {\n   134→            AllowImportRules::Yes\n   135→        };\n   136→        let new_rule = {\n   137→            let guard = parent_stylesheet.shared_lock.read();\n   138→            css_rules\n   139→                .read_with(&guard)\n   140→                .parse_rule_for_insert(\n   141→                    &parent_stylesheet.shared_lock,\n   142→                    &rule.str(),\n   143→                    parent_stylesheet.contents(&guard),\n   144→                    index,\n   145→                    containing_rule_types,\n   146→                    parse_relative_rule_type,\n   147→                    loader.as_ref().map(|l| l as &dyn StyleStylesheetLoader),\n   148→                    allow_import_rules,\n   149→                )\n   150→                .map_err(Convert::convert)?\n   151→        };\n   152→        {\n   153→            let mut guard = parent_stylesheet.shared_lock.write();\n   154→            css_rules\n   155→                .write_with(&mut guard)\n   156→                .0\n   157→                .insert(index, new_rule.clone());\n   158→        }\n   159→\n   160→        let parent_stylesheet = &*self.parent_stylesheet;\n   161→        parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   162→        let dom_rule = CSSRule::new_specific(window, parent_stylesheet, new_rule, can_gc);\n   163→        self.dom_rules\n   164→            .borrow_mut()\n   165→            .insert(index, MutNullableDom::new(Some(&*dom_rule)));\n   166→        parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   167→        Ok(idx)\n   168→    }\n   169→\n   170→    /// In case of a keyframe rule, index must be valid.\n   171→    pub(crate) fn remove_rule(&self, index: u32) -> ErrorResult {\n   172→        self.parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   173→\n   174→        let index = index as usize;\n   175→        let mut guard = self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock().write();\n   176→\n   177→        match *self.rules.borrow() {\n   178→            RulesSource::Rules(ref css_rules) => {\n   179→                css_rules\n   180→                    .write_with(&mut guard)\n   181→                    .remove_rule(index)\n   182→                    .map_err(Convert::convert)?;\n   183→                let mut dom_rules = self.dom_rules.borrow_mut();\n   184→                if let Some(r) = dom_rules[index].get() {\n   185→                    r.detach()\n   186→                }\n   187→                dom_rules.remove(index);\n   188→                self.parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   189→                Ok(())\n   190→            },\n   191→            RulesSource::Keyframes(ref kf) => {\n   192→                // https://drafts.csswg.org/css-animations/#dom-csskeyframesrule-deleterule\n   193→                let mut dom_rules = self.dom_rules.borrow_mut();\n   194→                if let Some(r) = dom_rules[index].get() {\n   195→                    r.detach()\n   196→                }\n   197→                dom_rules.remove(index);\n   198→                kf.write_with(&mut guard).keyframes.remove(index);\n   199→                self.parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   200→                Ok(())\n   201→            },\n   202→        }\n   203→    }\n   204→\n   205→    /// Remove parent stylesheets from all children\n   206→    pub(crate) fn deparent_all(&self) {\n   207→        for rule in self.dom_rules.borrow().iter() {\n   208→            if let Some(r) = rule.get() {\n   209→                DomRoot::upcast(r).deparent()\n   210→            }\n   211→        }\n   212→    }\n   213→\n   214→    pub(crate) fn item(&self, idx: u32, can_gc: CanGc) -> Option<DomRoot<CSSRule>> {\n   215→        self.dom_rules.borrow().get(idx as usize).map(|rule| {\n   216→            rule.or_init(|| {\n   217→                let parent_stylesheet = &self.parent_stylesheet;\n   218→                let lock = parent_stylesheet.shared_lock();\n   219→                match *self.rules.borrow() {\n   220→                    RulesSource::Rules(ref rules) => {\n   221→                        let rule = {\n   222→                            let guard = lock.read();\n   223→                            rules.read_with(&guard).0[idx as usize].clone()\n   224→                        };\n   225→                        CSSRule::new_specific(\n   226→                            self.global().as_window(),\n   227→                            parent_stylesheet,\n   228→                            rule,\n   229→                            can_gc,\n   230→                        )\n   231→                    },\n   232→                    RulesSource::Keyframes(ref rules) => {\n   233→                        let rule = {\n   234→                            let guard = lock.read();\n   235→                            rules.read_with(&guard).keyframes[idx as usize].clone()\n   236→                        };\n   237→                        DomRoot::upcast(CSSKeyframeRule::new(\n   238→                            self.global().as_window(),\n   239→                            parent_stylesheet,\n   240→                            rule,\n   241→                            can_gc,\n   242→                        ))\n   243→                    },\n   244→                }\n   245→            })\n   246→        })\n   247→    }\n   248→\n   249→    /// Add a rule to the list of DOM rules. This list is lazy,\n   250→    /// so we just append a placeholder.\n   251→    ///\n   252→    /// Should only be called for keyframes-backed rules, use insert_rule\n   253→    /// for CssRules-backed rules\n   254→    pub(crate) fn append_lazy_dom_rule(&self) {\n   255→        if let RulesSource::Rules(..) = &*self.rules.borrow() {\n   256→            panic!(\"Can only call append_lazy_rule with keyframes-backed CSSRules\");\n   257→        }\n   258→        self.dom_rules.borrow_mut().push(MutNullableDom::new(None));\n   259→    }\n   260→\n   261→    pub(super) fn update_rules(&self, rules: RulesSource, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\n   262→        let dom_rules = self.dom_rules.borrow();\n   263→        match rules {\n   264→            RulesSource::Rules(ref css_rules) => {\n   265→                if let RulesSource::Keyframes(..) = &*self.rules.borrow() {\n   266→                    panic!(\"Called update_rules on non-CssRule-backed CSSRuleList with CssRules\");\n   267→                }\n   268→\n   269→                let css_rules_iter = css_rules.read_with(guard).0.iter();\n   270→                for (dom_rule, css_rule) in izip!(dom_rules.iter(), css_rules_iter) {\n   271→                    let Some(dom_rule) = dom_rule.get() else {\n   272→                        continue;\n   273→                    };\n   274→                    dom_rule.update_rule(css_rule, guard);\n   275→                }\n   276→            },\n   277→            RulesSource::Keyframes(ref keyframesrule) => {\n   278→                if let RulesSource::Rules(..) = &*self.rules.borrow() {\n   279→                    panic!(\"Called update_rules on CssRule-backed CSSRuleList with non-CssRules\");\n   280→                }\n   281→\n   282→                let keyframerules_iter = keyframesrule.read_with(guard).keyframes.iter();\n   283→                for (dom_rule, keyframerule) in izip!(dom_rules.iter(), keyframerules_iter) {\n   284→                    let Some(dom_rule) = dom_rule.get() else {\n   285→                        continue;\n   286→                    };\n   287→                    let Some(dom_rule) = dom_rule.downcast::<CSSKeyframeRule>() else {\n   288→                        continue;\n   289→                    };\n   290→                    dom_rule.update_rule(keyframerule.clone(), guard);\n   291→                }\n   292→            },\n   293→        }\n   294→\n   295→        *self.rules.borrow_mut() = rules;\n   296→    }\n   297→}\n   298→\n   299→impl CSSRuleListMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSRuleList {\n   300→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#ref-for-dom-cssrulelist-item-1>\n   301→    fn Item(&self, idx: u32, can_gc: CanGc) -> Option<DomRoot<CSSRule>> {\n   302→        self.item(idx, can_gc)\n   303→    }\n   304→\n   305→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrulelist-length>\n   306→    fn Length(&self) -> u32 {\n   307→        self.dom_rules.borrow().len() as u32\n   308→    }\n   309→\n   310→    // check-tidy: no specs after this line\n   311→    fn IndexedGetter(&self, index: u32, can_gc: CanGc) -> Option<DomRoot<CSSRule>> {\n   312→        self.Item(index, can_gc)\n   313→    }\n   314→}\n   315→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it medialist.update_media_list(mediarule.media_queries.clone());\n        }\n        *self.mediarule.borrow_mut() = mediarule;\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SpecificCSSRule for CSSMediaRule {\n    fn ty(&self) ->
